query: void ab initio rescission order fraud

results.title: Infinitum Holding (Pty) Ltd and Another v Lerm and Others (26799/2017) [2022] ZALCJHB 1 (18 May 2022)
metadata: Labour Court of South Africa, Johannesburg
results.summary: Rescission refused; business rescue resolution not void ab initio and estoppel upheld against attackers of the settlement.
results.topics: Companies Act — business rescue — s129(1), s129(2)(a) and s130 — resolution to commence business rescue not void ab initio when liquidation proceedings exist; remedy is application to set aside under s130; Rescission — Uniform Rule 42(1)(a) and common law — requirements and discretion; Authority of attorneys — apparent authority and estoppel; Delay and costs — inordinate delay and vexatious conduct may justify de bonis propriis costs.

results.title: Black Sheep Capital (Pty) Ltd v Du Toit and Others (2021/35297) [2023] ZAGPJHC 1541 (21 December 2023)
metadata: High Court of South Africa South Gauteng, Johannesburg
results.summary: Plaintiff's bare plea that agreements were void ab initio was excipiable; particulars were vague and amendment ordered.
results.topics: Pleadings – Rule 18(4) – material facts must be pleaded with sufficient particularity; facta probanda vs facta probantia; exception for vagueness and embarrassment; cannot plead bare legal conclusion that agreements are void ab initio; reliance on alleged admissions in other proceedings insufficient; leave to amend and costs ordered.

results.title: NW Civil Contractors CC v Anton Ramaano Inc and Another (1024/2018; 1076/2018) [2019] ZASCA 143 (14 October 2019)
metadata: Supreme Court of Appeal of South Africa
results.summary: Lack of a fidelity fund certificate disentitles an attorney to fees and creates criminal liability but does not render proceedings void ab initio.
results.topics: Attorneys Act s41(1) – fidelity fund certificate – consequence of practising without certificate – statutory disentitlement to fees (s41(2)) and criminal sanction (s83(10)) – non‑compliance does not render proceedings void ab initio; court orders must be precise and not overbroad or vague; rescission of default judgment – reasonable explanation and bona fide defence.

results.title: Minister of Safety and Security v Phakula and Others (HCA 19/2015) [2018] ZAGPPHC 515 (22 February 2018)
metadata: High Court of South Africa North Gauteng, Pretoria
results.summary: Consent order procured without attorney's mandate was voidable for fraud; rescission granted and punitive costs awarded.
results.topics: Magistrates' Courts Act s36(1)(b) – rescission where judgment void ab origine or obtained by fraud; consent judgments; mandate and locus standi. Consent orders – vitiation by fraud; ex post facto powers of attorney do not validate lack of original mandate. Attorney conduct – lack of collective power of attorney; touting and improper representation; punitive costs (attorney-and-client, two counsel). Res judicata – finality of consent orders subject to rescission where procured by fraud

results.title: Titan Asset Management (Pty) Ltd and Others v Lanzerac Estate Investments (Pty) Ltd and Another (2102 / 2020) [2023] ZAWCHC 76 (9 June 2023)
metadata: High Court of South Africa Western Cape, Cape Town
results.summary: Fraudulent non-disclosure permits rescission despite contractual clauses; adequacy of restitution and non-joinder are triable matters.
results.topics:
Contract law – rescission for fraud – fraudulent non-disclosure renders contract void ab initio and exclusion clauses cannot bar rescission for fraud
Restitution – adequacy of tender – substituted or post-scheme shares and fungibility of listed shares may suffice; adequacy is a triable issue. Civil procedure – exception vs special plea – non-joinder objection is generally a special plea unless pleading on its face lacks necessary averments; exception appropriate only where want of cause of action or evident lack of standing appears ex facie the pleadings

results.title: Courtney v Boshoff NNO and Others (483/2023) [2024] ZASCA 104 (21 June 2024)
metadata: Supreme Court of Appeal of South Africa
results.summary: Final sequestration without a prior provisional order is not automatically void; rescission requires proper grounds, absence or delay bars relief.
results.topics: Insolvency law – final sequestration granted without prior provisional order – timing irregularity not necessarily void ab initio; rescission remedies – rule 42(1)(a) and common-law rescission – necessity to show absence of wilful default and good cause; distinction from cases where court usurped statutory power (Motala; Knoop).

results.title: North East Finance (Pty) Ltd v Standard Bank of South Africa Ltd (492/2012) [2013] ZASCA 76 (29 May 2013)
metadata: Supreme Court of Appeal of South Africa
results.summary: An arbitration clause will not bind the respondent where the contract appears induced by fraud; separability depends on context.
results.topics: Arbitration law – Separability and effect of fraud – An arbitration clause embedded in a contract may fall with the contract if the contract is void ab initio, but separability depends on clause construction and contextual commercial purpose. Contract interpretation – Contextual approach – "Enforceability" may cover validity, but whether fraud-induced invalidity is arbitrable depends on the parties’ intended scope of the clause and the factual matrix. Civil procedure – Referral to arbitration – A court need not compel arbitration where fraud allegations on the papers are not wholly unfounded and raise disputed facts

results.title: Steenkamp N.O. and Others v Rampai and Others (2478/2022) [2023] ZAFSHC 112 (15 June 2023)
metadata: High Court of South Africa Free State, Bloemfontein
results.summary: Property registration procured by fraudulent misrepresentation is void ab initio and must be cancelled; estate registration authorized.
results.topics: Deeds registries — cancellation of title procured by fraud; Fraud vitiates transactions — registration void ab initio; Intestate succession — property reverting to estate after setting aside of improper transfer; Procedural — misjoinder objection dismissed; Relief — court may authorize registration in estate and empower officers/attorneys to sign.

results.title: Special Investigating Unit v Gibela Investments (Pty) Ltd and Another (KN02/2020) [2021] ZAST 5 (18 May 2021)
results.summary: Tribunal set aside a tender award, declared the contract void ab initio, and ordered accounting and disgorgement with interest.
results.topics: Procurement law – administrative review of tender award – award set aside; contract declared void ab initio; restitutionary relief – accounting, disgorgement of profits and interest.

results.title: FirstRand Bank Ltd v McLachlan and Others (394/2019) [2020] ZASCA 31 (1 April 2020)
metadata: Supreme Court of Appeal of South Africa
results.summary: Debt review courts may not vary contractual interest or order instalments below monthly interest; such orders are void.
results.topics: National Credit Act – Debt review – s 86(7)(c)(ii) powers – re-arrangement may not alter underlying contractual obligations or vary agreed interest rates; re-arrangement producing monthly instalments less than monthly interest is ultra vires and void ab origine – rescission of void debt review order competent – rescission interlocutory and not appealable.

results.title: South African Broadcasting Corporation SOC Ltd and Another v Lornavision (Pty) Ltd (49514/2017) [2024] ZAGPJHC 1600 (19 March 2024)
metadata: High Court of South Africa South Gauteng, Johannesburg
results.summary: Default judgment granted ordering restitution of R62,733,556.61 paid under a contract declared void ab initio.
results.topics: Default judgment — absence of plea; restitution for unjust enrichment/no-profit where contract void ab initio; admissibility of affidavits despite commissioning objections; parallel litigation/ consolidation not a defence to default judgment; par delictum rule not pleaded and made academic by restitution principles (Allpay, Shabangu).

results.title: FirstRand Bank Limited v Vellem ; In re: Vellem v Firstrand Bank Limited (M404/2022) [2025] ZANWHC 106 (12 June 2025)
metadata: High Court of South Africa North-West, Mafikeng
results.summary: Rescission granted where default judgment was obtained against the wrong entity and service was defective.
results.topics: Rule 42(1)(a) – Rescission of judgment erroneously granted; Incorrect citation of juristic person; Defective service at branch without authority; Judgment in favour of person not before court; Proper service at registered office; Audi alteram partem and void ab initio doctrine; Costs in rescission – costs in the cause.

results.title: Bilankulu v Mtshali and Others ; In Re: Isabel v Bilankulu and Others (111218/2023) [2024] ZAGPJHC 1824 (25 July 2024)
metadata: High Court of South Africa South Gauteng, Johannesburg
results.summary: Rescission refused: prior customary marriage renders later civil marriage void; personal service and default judgment upheld.
results.topics: Family law — Recognition of Customary Marriages Act — effect of pre-existing customary marriage on subsequent civil marriage; civil marriage during subsistence of customary marriage void ab initio; rescission of default judgment — proof of personal service, good cause and bona fide defence required; exercise of discretion on costs where outcome may be inequitable.

results.title: Malapela and Another v Zondo and Another (2023-065428) [2025] ZAGPJHC 471 (11 June 2025)
metadata: High Court of South Africa South Gauteng, Johannesburg
results.summary: Default judgment rescinded because the loan was an unlawful, void-ab-initio credit agreement under the National Credit Act; bar uplifted.
results.topics: National Credit Act – Unregistered credit provider – s40/s42 threshold set at R nil – credit agreement (interest from earlier date) not incidental – s89(2)(d) and s89(5) – agreement void ab initio; Civil procedure – Rule 42(1)(a) rescission of judgment erroneously sought/granted; Rule 27 – upliftment of bar and leave to file plea.

results.title: Bridge Debt (Pty) Ltd and Another v Mothibi (CA&R34/23) [2025] ZANCHC 2 (14 March 2025)
metadata: High Court of South Africa Northern Cape, Kimberley
results.summary: Whether a judgment and emoluments attachment order obtained in an incorrect magistrate's district are void ab origine and who must make restitution and pay costs.
results.topics: • Magistrates' Courts Act s 45 – consent to jurisdiction; judgment must be entered in court agreed by parties.  • Emoluments attachment orders – must be issued from court of debtor’s residence, business or employment; orders obtained in wrong forum void ab origine.  • Rescission (s 36(1)(b)) – void judgments may be rescinded and parties restored to status quo ante.  • Restitution – rescindable benefits restituted to debtor; attorneys only liable if they received rescindable benefit.  • Costs – attorney-and-client costs warranted where attorney acted improperly in forum-shopping or caused prejudice.
